Class Hero

java.lang.Object
Model.Hero
public class Hero extends Object
Represents the player-controlled hero character in the game. The hero can move, place bombs, take damage, collect points, and manage power-up effects. It uses JavaFX IntegerProperty for lives and score to facilitate UI binding. * @author Luis Carlos/ Nathan Ng

    Property
    Description

    Lives
    Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's lives.
    javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty
    Score
    Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's score.

  • Property Details

    • Lives

      public javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty LivesProperty
      Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's lives. This is used for binding the lives count to a UI element.
      See Also:
      • getLives()
      • setLives(int)
      • LivesProperty()

    • Score

      public javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty ScoreProperty
      Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's score. This is used for binding the score count to a UI element.
      See Also:
      • getScore()
      • setScore(int)
      • ScoreProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • Hero

      public Hero(String name)
      Constructs a new Hero with a specified name. Initializes lives to 3, score to 0, sets the hero as alive, and sets the base bomb blast radius.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the hero character.

  • Method Details

    • Move

      public void Move(char input, Map map, ArrayList<Enemy> enemies, ArrayList<Snake> snakes)
      Moves the hero according to player input and handles map interactions. The hero can move up ('w'), down ('s'), left ('a'), or right ('d') if the destination cell is walkable and within bounds. The 'f' key is used to place a bomb. Movement onto the 'T' (Treasure) cell is blocked if any enemy is still alive.
      Parameters:
      input - The player's input character ('w', 'a', 's', 'd', or 'f'). // Added missing tag
      map - The game map that defines valid movement and object positions.
      enemies - A list of all active enemies in the current level.
      snakes - A list of all active snake enemies in the current level.

    • PlaceBomb

      public Bomb PlaceBomb(Map map, ArrayList<Enemy> enemies, ArrayList<Snake> snakes)
      Places a new Bomb at the hero’s current position, if a bomb does not already exist at the location. The bomb is initialized with the hero's current blast radius and starts its countdown. The bomb is visualized as 'B' on the map.
      Parameters:
      map - The map where the bomb will be placed. // Added missing tag
      enemies - The list of enemies affected by the bomb.
      snakes - The list of snakes affected by the bomb.
      Returns:
      The newly created Bomb object, or null if a bomb is already present.

    • TakeDamage

      public void TakeDamage()
      Reduces the hero's lives by 1 when they take damage. If the hero's lives drop to 0 or below, the Die() method is called. This method also implicitly resets the bomb blast radius to the base value after damage is taken, as per typical power-up rules (though the reset logic is often handled externally or within this method).

    • Die

      public void Die()
      Sets the hero's isAlive status to false and triggers end-game logic. This method should only be called if the hero's life count is 0 or less.

    • increaseBombBlastRadius

      public void increaseBombBlastRadius(int amount)
      Increases the hero's bomb blast radius by the specified amount. This is typically called when a Blast Radius Power-Up is collected.
      Parameters:
      amount - The value to add to the current bomb blast radius. // Added missing tag

    • resetBombBlastRadius

      public void resetBombBlastRadius()
      Resets the bomb blast radius to the BASE_RADIUS. This is typically called when the hero takes damage.

    • getBombBlastRadius

      public int getBombBlastRadius()
      Gets the hero's current bomb blast radius. This value is used by the Bomb constructor when placing a new bomb.
      Returns:
      The current bomb blast radius (e.g., 2 for base, 3 or more if powered up).

    • increaseLife

      public void increaseLife(int amount)
      Increases the hero's life count by the specified amount. This is typically called when an ExtraLife power-up is collected.
      Parameters:
      amount - The number of lives to add.

    • getPositionX

      public int getPositionX()
      Gets the current x-coordinate of the hero.
      Returns:
      The x-coordinate.

    • getPositionY

      public int getPositionY()
      Gets the current y-coordinate of the hero.
      Returns:
      The y-coordinate.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Gets the name of the hero.
      Returns:
      The hero's name.

    • getIsAlive

      public boolean getIsAlive()
      Checks if the hero is currently alive.
      Returns:
      true if the hero's life count is greater than 0, false otherwise.

    • LivesProperty

      public javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty LivesProperty()
      Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's lives. This is used for binding the lives count to a UI element.
      Returns:
      The bindable property for lives.
      See Also:
      • getLives()
      • setLives(int)

    • ScoreProperty

      public javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty ScoreProperty()
      Gets the JavaFX IntegerProperty for the hero's score. This is used for binding the score count to a UI element.
      Returns:
      The bindable property for score.
      See Also:
      • getScore()
      • setScore(int)

    • getScore

      public int getScore()
      Gets the current score of the hero.
      Returns:
      The current score value.

    • getLives

      public int getLives()
      Gets the current number of lives the hero has.
      Returns:
      The current lives count.

    • addScore

      public void addScore(int value)
      Adds a specified value to the current score.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to add to the current score.

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(int x, int y)
      Sets the hero's position to the specified coordinates.
      Parameters:
      x - The new X coordinate.
      y - The new Y coordinate.

    • setScore

      public void setScore(int score)
      Sets the hero's score to a specific value.
      Parameters:
      score - The new score value.

    • setLives

      public void setLives(int lives)
      Sets the hero's lives to a specific value.
      Parameters:
      lives - The new lives count.
